title: Three-act screenplay analysis
summary: Urban has a strong moral engine, but the opening can make his active choice clearer.

criteria.label: Dramatic Energy
criteria.description: How strongly the moment pulls the audience forward.

criteria.label: Stakes
criteria.description: How clearly the audience understands what can be lost or gained.

criteria.label: Character Agency
criteria.description: How clearly a character's choice drives the story.

acts.title: The Offer
acts.synopsis: Urban is introduced through need, skill, and the first failed patronage attempt.

critique.summary: The act has pressure, but Urban's active choice can arrive sooner.
strengths: The premise gives craft a moral consequence.
concerns: The opening pressure can feel external before Urban's want is explicit.

evidence.text: The first scene shows pressure before it clearly dramatizes Urban choosing a path.

suggestions: Make Urban's decision pressure visible before the act moves to negotiation.

acts.title: The Patron
acts.synopsis: Urban enters a world that can fund his ambition and shrink his moral distance.

critique.summary: The act gains scale when resources meet ambition.
strengths: The patron relationship naturally raises stakes.
concerns: Urban's self-justification should be tested by visible human cost.

evidence.text: The patron scene turns skill into political consequence.

suggestions: Let a scene force Urban to defend his bargain in human terms.

acts.title: The Sound
acts.synopsis: The weapon works, and Urban must face the meaning of success.

critique.summary: The final act has scale and consequence; the strongest version makes recognition unavoidable.
strengths: The climax naturally ties invention to empire-level cost.
concerns: Urban's inner recognition needs a clear dramatic expression.

evidence.text: The wall sequence shows the weapon succeeding at a human cost.

suggestions: Give Urban a concrete moment of recognition before the final consequence lands.

keyBeats.label: Hook
keyBeats.synopsis: The story opens on pressure created by Urban's craft.

critique.summary: The hook is forceful but can attach more tightly to Urban's choice.

evidence.text: The scene shows consequence before choice.

suggestions: Tie the opening image to a question about Urban's responsibility.

sequences.title: The Sound That Opens Stone
sequences.synopsis: The sequence frames the cost of the weapon before the bargain.

critique.summary: A strong pressure sequence with room for clearer agency.
strengths: The audience understands that the weapon matters.
concerns: The audience needs a sharper sense of Urban's immediate want.

evidence.text: The scene emphasizes result before decision.

suggestions: End the sequence on a question about responsibility, not only spectacle.

scenes.title: Bombardment
scenes.synopsis: The cannon's impact establishes the cost of Urban's craft.

critique.summary: The scene is forceful but low on visible agency.
strengths: Immediate consequence gives the story scale.
concerns: Urban is defined by effect before choice.

evidence.text: The conflict is external before Urban's decision is dramatized.

suggestions: Add or revise a beat so Urban actively confronts what his craft enables.

suggestedSceneAdditions.title: The Maker Calculates
suggestedSceneAdditions.purpose: Give Urban an active choice after the opening pressure.
suggestedSceneAdditions.synopsis: Urban privately weighs whether his craft can survive without patronage and chooses pursuit over retreat.
suggestedSceneAdditions.rationale: This makes the hook personal instead of only situational.

expectedCriterionChanges.reason: The audience sees Urban choose pressure rather than merely experience it.
